data.title: Polish crypto firms struggle with uncertainty amid discussions on delayed law
data.description:
Polish crypto companies are grappling with persisting uncertainty about the future of their industry amid continuing debates over a long-awaited bill to regulate the digital assets market in the country. The delayed adoption of the legislation designed to transpose European rules into national law puts Poland in a shrinking group of member states that are […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/polish-firms-struggle-with-delayed-law/

data.title: Analysts: Tight monetary policy will drag down US economic outlook
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News SignatureFD analyst Tony Welch said that the decline in U.S. Treasury yields after the Fed meeting may indicate that investors are worried about the impact of tight monetary policy on the economy. The economy is currently slowing down, and implementing tighter monetary policy for a longer period of time will definitely be a disadvantage. Trump's tariffs are affecting the Fed's thinking because the FOMC statement mentioned trade "at the beginning." Powell will use the word "uncertainty" many times in the press conference. (Jinshi)

data.title: Powell Warns of Tariff Impact on Inflation and Employment
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell has expressed concerns over the significant impact of tariffs, which have been larger than anticipated. He stated that if the announced tariff increases persist, it could lead to higher inflation and lower employment rates. The ability to avoid sustained inflation will depend on the scale, duration, and inflation expectations related to these tariffs.

data.title: Powell talks about the recent rise in inflation expectations: tariffs are the main factor
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell said at a press conference that recent inflation expectations indicators have risen, reflected in market and survey-based indicators. Respondents, including consumers, businesses and professional forecasters, pointed out that tariffs are a driving factor. However, after the next year or so, most long-term expectation indicators remain consistent with our 2% inflation target. (Jinshi)

data.title: Powell: It is impossible to immediately determine which is more important, inflation or unemployment
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Powell was asked which task needs to be solved first - higher inflation or unemployment. He said that both risks have increased. It is not clear which one will be a bigger concern. He said, "It is too early to draw conclusions now." (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed's Powell: Rate cuts this year would be appropriate in some cases
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell said that in some cases it is appropriate to cut interest rates this year, and in some cases it is inappropriate to cut interest rates. I cannot confidently say that I know the appropriate interest rate path. (Jinshi)

data.title: Powell: The economy is in a solid state and inflation has dropped significantly
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell said that the economy is in a solid state and inflation has dropped significantly. The current inflation rate is slightly above the target level of 2%, and the labor market has reached or is close to the maximum employment level. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed Chair Powell Reports Economic Stability and Inflation Decline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has stated that the economy is in a stable condition, with inflation having significantly decreased. The current inflation rate is slightly above the 2% target level, and the labor market has reached or is near maximum employment.

data.title: Fed Chairman Powell: If the sharp increase in tariffs continues, there will be higher inflation and lower employment
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell: Tariffs have been much larger than expected so far. If the sharp increase in tariffs as announced continues, there will be higher inflation and lower employment. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed's Powell: Inflationary effects of policy may be short-lived
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell: Avoiding persistent inflation will depend on the scale, timing and inflation expectations of tariffs, and the inflationary effect of the policy may be short-lived. (Jinshi)

data.title: Former Fed Vice Chairman: Markets have priced in a 40% to 50% chance of a recession
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Former Fed Vice Chairman Clarida said that the market has priced in the possibility of a recession between 40% and 50%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Futures Contracts Linked to Fed Policy Rates Show Increased Rate Cut Probability
data.description: According to Odaily, futures contracts tied to the Federal Reserve's policy rates have rebounded following the announcement of the interest rate decision. The current pricing indicates a 30% probability that the Fed will cut rates by 25 basis points as early as June, slightly higher than the earlier estimate of 27%. Based on futures prices, there is approximately a 75% chance of a rate cut by July.

data.title: Former Fed Vice Chair Clarida Discusses Recession Probability
data.description: According to Odaily, former Federal Reserve Vice Chair Richard Clarida has stated that the market has priced in the likelihood of an economic recession at between 40% and 50%.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Continues Downward Trend as Nasdaq Falls
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. stock market has continued its downward trend, with the Nasdaq Index declining by 1.00%. This movement reflects ongoing volatility in the market, as investors navigate various economic factors influencing stock performance.

data.title: Fed remains on hold, stressing rising risks to unemployment and inflation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged for the third consecutive meeting and emphasized that they believe the risks of rising inflation and unemployment are increasing. The FOMC committee said, "Uncertainty about the economic outlook has further increased. The committee is paying attention to the risks facing its dual mission in both directions and judges that the risks of high unemployment and high inflation have increased." Officials voted unanimously to keep interest rates in the range of 4.25%-4.5%. The Fed said it would continue to shrink its balance sheet at the pace announced at the March meeting. Trump's trade policy has triggered a wave of uncertainty throughout the economy. While tariffs are still under negotiation, economists generally expect that comprehensive tariffs will push up inflation and drag down economic growth. This will put the two goals of policymakers (price stability and maximizing employment) in conflict with each other. (Jinshi)

data.title: The probability of a Fed rate cut in July is about 75%.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Futures contracts tied to the Fed's policy rate recovered their earlier losses after the interest rate decision was announced, and the current pricing is that the Fed will cut interest rates by 25 basis points as early as June at a 30% probability, slightly higher than the earlier 27%. According to futures prices, the probability of a rate cut by July is about 75%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analyst: Job market will eventually push Fed to cut rates
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Neil Dutta of Renaissance Macro believes that the job market will eventually drive the Fed's pace of rate cuts. He said: "Labor market conditions have cooled. Why does the Fed think this will stabilize on its own? It can't and won't, which means that policy responses will eventually be needed." (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Highlights Increased Uncertainty in Economic Outlook
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the Federal Reserve reiterated its focus on the risks associated with its dual mandate in a recent FOMC statement. The statement emphasized that uncertainty regarding the economic outlook has "further increased." While the unemployment rate has stabilized and the labor market remains resilient, the risks of high unemployment and inflation have risen. Despite fluctuations in net exports affecting data, economic activity continues to expand at a "robust pace."

data.title: Traders Anticipate Federal Reserve Rate Cuts by July
data.description: According to BlockBeats, following the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate decision, traders continue to expect a rate cut by July. The market still anticipates three rate reductions within this year.

data.title: Federal Reserve Acknowledges Economic Challenges Amid GDP Decline
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve officials noted in their May policy statement that the GDP growth for the first quarter turned negative. Despite this, the Federal Reserve's policy committee still considers the underlying economy to be strong. They identified significant fluctuations in trade statistics as the primary cause for the GDP contraction in the first quarter, with imports surging ahead of new tariffs. The statement highlighted that, although net export volatility affected the data, recent indicators suggest that economic activity continues to expand at a robust pace. However, the new language in this month's policy statement also acknowledges that the risks of rising unemployment and inflation have intensified.
